figures.caption: Table 1: Finetuning results on GSM8K for Llama-2 7B and Llama-3 1B. For Llama-2 7B, OASIS matches the performance of Adam while reducing peak memory by ∼ 2×.

Figure 2: Memory breakdown across training components for different methods on LLaMA2 7B GSM8K finetuning at rank 32. OASIS reduces memory across activations, gradients, and optimizer states, leading to the lowest overall memory footprint.

Figure 1: Memory breakdown and subspace dynamics during training. (a) As batch size and sequence length scale, memory becomes increasingly dominated by activations. (b) These activations lie in subspaces that evolve continuously during training.

Table 2: Finetuning results on HumanEval for Llama-2 7B (trained on CodeAlpaca). OASIS achieves the best performance among low-rank methods at aggressive compression, and remains competitive with the strongest baseline at higher rank, while consistently using the lowest memory.
Table 4: Hyperparameters for finetuning experiments on GSM8K and HumanEval.
Table 5: Hyperparameters for pretraining experiments on C4 presented in Table 3.

Figure 3: Subspace drift during pretraining on C4 for Llama-130M and Llama-350M. Both models exhibit a transient phase followed by stabilization at a non-zero drift, indicating that the activation subspace evolves throughout training.

Figure 4: Comparison of periodic PCA and OASIS during finetuning on GSM8K with Llama2 7B. (a) Periodic PCA consistently underperforms OASIS across all tested update intervals. (b) OASIS also converges faster, achieving lower training loss compared to periodic PCA.
